scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope production reporting produced explicit metric definitions, source precedence, caveats, and submission logic grounded in batch, lot, release, downtime, and supplier records.
The disputed yield denominator was blocked until the tenant definition and source hierarchy were clarified.
Missing MES, WMS, and batch-close records triggered a low-confidence escalation instead of an unsupported published trend.
ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS disagreements were preserved as a reconciliation case with no single authoritative production figure asserted.
Unsupported causal and safety-sensitive claims were refused and routed with the evidence gaps stated explicitly.
Incomplete close-period inputs and unresolved release tie-outs blocked report submission and produced an evidence-backed escalation package.
Traceability-aware analysis preserved batch and lot lineage, transformation context, and release posture without overstating genealogy confidence.
Held output was kept distinct from released output and the lane escalated to quality owners instead of bypassing the release boundary.
Supplier substitution pressure was summarized but not approved, and the case was routed to supplier-quality and procurement owners.
Engineering and rework authority were rejected cleanly and the reporting evidence package was preserved for handoff.
Missing calendar, source-precedence, release-authority, and retrieval-hook facts produced a blocked-prerequisites response instead of implied comparability.
Conflicting tenant policy and release controls were preserved as an escalation case instead of being merged into a false committed-output answer.
Tenant-specific schedule-attainment adaptation was recorded explicitly while preserving the baseline definition and comparison posture.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to food-and-beverage production reporting, source tie-outs, traceability-aware reconciliation, hold and release boundaries, and disclosure-safe caveat handling.

Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the functional ability contract.
